description: Position-momentum uncertainty measurements from various quantum systems. Demonstrates Heisenberg's uncertainty principle: Δx·Δp ≥ ℏ/2. Data from three experimental techniques: (1) single-slit diffraction: electron beam through slits of varying width a, where Δx ≈ a/√12 and Δp measured from diffraction pattern width; (2) ion trap cooling limits: ground-state cooled ions showing minimum uncertainty state with Δx·Δp = ℏ/2; (3) photon entanglement: SPDC photon pairs with correlated position-momentum measurements. Error bars represent combined systematic and statistical uncertainties in measurement precision. References: Nairz et al. (2002) fullerene diffraction, Monroe et al. (1996) ion trap, Howell et al. (2004) entanglement.
